perturbation.original_text: 9.5 Audit Rights. Each party will have the right to engage an independent third party to audit the books and records of the other party relevant to the calculation of Retail Impressions or CNET Sales, upon reasonable notice and during normal business hours, and the other party will provide reasonable cooperation in connection with any such audit. The party requesting the audit will pay all expenses of the auditor unless the audit reveals an underpayment by the other party of more than 5%, in which case the other party will reimburse all reasonable expenses of the auditor.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.5 Audit Rights. Each party will have the right to engage an independent third party to audit the books and records of the other party relevant to the calculation of Retail Impressions or CNET Sales, upon reasonable notice and during normal business hours, and the other party will provide reasonable cooperation in connection with any such audit. The party requesting the audit will pay all expenses of the auditor unless the audit reveals an underpayment by the other party of more than 10%, in which case the other party will reimburse all reasonable expenses of the auditor.
perturbation.explanation: The threshold for the underpayment that triggers reimbursement of audit expenses is changed from '5%' to '10%'. This creates an in-text contradiction as it alters the financial obligation related to audit findings within Section 9.5, leading to ambiguity on which threshold should be enforced, thus affecting the financial responsibilities of the parties. The contradiction resides because the contract now dictates two different underpayment thresholds in the same audit rights clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.5 Audit Rights. Each party will have the right to engage an independent third party to audit the books and records of the other party relevant to the calculation of Retail Impressions or CNET Sales, upon reasonable notice and during normal business hours, and the other party will provide reasonable cooperation in connection with any such audit. The party requesting the audit will pay all expenses of the auditor unless the audit reveals an underpayment by the other party of more than 10%, in which case the other party will reimburse all reasonable expenses of the auditor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the threshold for reimbursement of audit expenses from 5% to 10%. The contradicted text contains the modified version (10%). However, the perturbation explanation states the original text was changed from 5% to 10%. Since the contradicted text retains the modified clause (10%), while the changed text created a contradiction by going from 5% to 10%, there is a contradiction. The original section stated 5% and the new section states 10% creating a direct conflict regarding the threshold, and thus, impacting financial obligations. This leads to an inconsistency and in-text contradiction within the document because it presents conflicting financial obligations, which violates the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as the change introduces different penalties, obligations, and deadlines.
